sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.109 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: A subgroup$H$of a finite group$G$is called pronormal if for any$g G$the subgroups$H$and$H^g$are conjugate by an element of$H,H^g$. A maximal subgroup of a maximal subgroup is called second maximal. Is it true that in a non-abelian finite simple group$G$all maximal subgroups are Hall subgroups if and only if every second maximal subgroup of$G$is pronormal in$G$? { V.\,I.\,Zenkov} No, not always, fo...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
